threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still use ‘The Dalston’ instead of just ‘Dalston.’ Locals dropped the ‘The’ ages ago; you’ll catch on soon enough.
Last night on the 59, overheard a bloke trying to convince a girl that his old Nokia was a vintage phone – she was not having it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The smell of fresh döner and currywurst wafting through the streets at 2 AM after a night out, it's a reminder that this place never sleeps.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you get a currywurst served from a converted fire truck at 3 AM, surrounded by street art and laughter. It’s a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their ability to navigate BART delays without breaking a sweat—like knowing which bus lines to hop over when the trains mess up.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full suit balancing a coffee and his laptop while the Muni jolted—he never spilled a drop. Classic San Francisco multitasking!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the plazas—miss the hidden art in the alleys and the locals' murals. It's a living canvas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still ask for 'una tapa' instead of just diving in and sharing plates like everyone else. You can spot them at the local bars, for sure.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can tell a newbie by how they look for the 243 bus stop instead of just walking the two blocks to the train. Classic rookie mistake.
Caught the N68 last night, and the driver started singing along to classic soul. Had the whole back of the bus in stitches. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids trading Pokémon cards on the sidewalk while a couple argued passionately in Turkish next door. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know it’s Berlin when you see a crowd of people dancing on the U-Bahn platform during a strike. The city's chaos has a certain rhythm!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know the best taco trucks have lines that start well before noon, while newcomers just hit the trendy taquerias they read about online.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy arguing with the Muni driver about the route while juggling a pizza. Classic SF chaos, you can’t make this stuff up.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are for relaxing, but locals know they're just waiting areas for fútbol fans during match days.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still struggling with the ‘hola’ and ‘gràcies’ while fumbling with their mapa, lost in the winding alleys of Gràcia.
